replies.text: ​@540VarialHunterSA was the name of their organisation or Sturmabteilung. "Brown shirts" is how they are described today and also a way to describe any violent front of any fascistic and/or authoritarian political movement. Like the modern left with their enforcer Antifa.

replies.author: @VanTesla
replies.text:
​@retromeister3709I think perhaps you got hyper-focused and confused. Her mom is bad, but her dad is good. She only mentioned her mom's former neo-ze boyfriend who was after her dad.

Her dad was unfairly treated by the courts regarding child custody, which he fought to gain, giving information shared from Kirsche in past streams. Kirsche's mother manipulated the system, essentially kidnapping her for a few years when the courts initially granted split custody.  Later, in her teens, Kirsche was given to her grandparents, who are good people and closer to her father. She maintains constant contact with her father, and he is currently helping her find a house closer to her good family. She does not contact her mother, and the last she heard from her was when her mother pretended to be dead.

replies.text: ​@DarthBlackheart frog. And the frog wasn't resistant, they both die.

replies.author: @stephenfitzgerald9769
replies.text: @KeepTheKitLow very true. But I think that the OP is posting with the knowledge outside of the original fable that many frogs and toads are resistant to the venom of creatures that they regularly consume. Confusing, if you’re only aware of the fable and not the factoid, so it probably wasn’t the best metaphor to use. Still apt, though…

replies.author: @KeepTheKitLow
replies.text:
@stephenfitzgerald9769  nah, confusing regardless. 

Factoids are fun, but using half a fable and then branching off into one seems like misinterpretation of the fable. More brain rot :)

Also: Many (all?) frogs and toads get their chemicals from what they eat and are not inherently poisonous, ants and the like give it to them. Few have resistance to scorpions, not all have poison resistance.

Edit: venom =/= poison, poison is ingested (or touched), venom is injected. Very few frogs (if any) have venom resistance, many (relatively) have poison resistance. I get it, factoids can be confusing.

replies.author: @stephenfitzgerald9769
replies.text: @  touché. Also, thank you for citing the difference between venom and poison; I run into a lot of people who don’t know or care about the difference…

replies.author: @RiskOfBaer
replies.text: You must not play many video games anymore if you honestly believe that. Expand your horizons, I have more great games than I have time to play and I actively avoid the whole AAA industry, anything with a hint of woke, and general mediocrity. I pretty much only play games that are exceptional in one way or another, and there is STILL too many of them. My backlog only keeps growing.

replies.author: @MSAPtube
replies.text: @RiskOfBaer  I wouldn't be surprised if the new I, Robot remake by Llamasoft and Atari is the last video game I ever buy. Nothing else will ever compare. Maybe if someone comes out with a really good Shmup, I could be tempted. But I'm not into sitting there bored and watching cut scenes just to marvel over my graphics card.

replies.author: @snoozeflu
replies.text: Same with me. I am slowly losing any enthusiasm and passion for video games in general anymore. If anything, I will go into my back catalog and revisit older games, like from the PS3 / XBOX 360 era (and earlier) and play those. They are still really good games with none of the BS.

replies.author: @RiskOfBaer
replies.text:
@MSAPtube See, that's what I mean. It seems you believe all modern gaming amounts to is watching cut scenes and photorealistic graphics. Fortunately you couldn't be more wrong thinking that. 

You clearly have a very narrow understanding of what video games are and can actually be. That's why I recommended expanding your horizons, there are so many great games out there from so many genres that you've never even tried! So many game developers you've never heard of. Try new things, something you'd never consider before. If you limit yourself to only that which you find familiar (Which usually means western AAA industry) then yeah, of course things will probably be looking bleak right now. 

An easy place to start is to simply open Steam and sort by best rated games of all time, I guarantee you that there will be plenty of exceptional games even in the top 100 that you've never player or known about. Well, provided you actually want to try them, I've seen plenty of people who complain there are no good games anymore but they simply refuse to even consider games like Terraria, Hollow Knight, Rimworld, Factorio, To the Moon, Stardew Valley, Ultrakill, Slime Rancher, or many other fantastic video games because of various biases they have: oh it looks dumb, oh I don't like platformers, oh it has pixel graphics, etcetera. Basically they don't actually want to play a goop video games, they want something familiar that they are used to. And with that attitude... you're not going to be finding a lot of good games I'm afraid. 

But as long as you are open to new experiences and unfamiliar ground, you'll find them very easily, in fact there will be too many of them for you to even play and you'd have to be picky with your time.

replies.author: @MSAPtube
replies.text: @RiskOfBaer  sorry if I didn't want to play boring games. Go play Dodonpachi SaiDaiOuJou and then recommend me another gay cozy game. The platformers are just crap. Ori and all that shit looks the same. Why don't you EVEN CONSIDER SHMUPS or retro Atari or the new I ROBOT??? LOL. I can't believe you even brought up terraria. I need DENSE GAME PLAY, not a slow morphine drip.

replies.author: @RiskOfBaer
@MSAPtube
